type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Our features both touch quantize()'s signature in the same files, so a disjoint split isn't possible. Let's each implement independently on our own branch (I'll add error_threshold, you add sort_palette), keep our additions as separate, additive parameters/blocks to minimize conflicts, and rely on git merge at the end to combine both. I'll proceed now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Our features both touch quantize() in the same files, so full disjointness isn't possible. Accepting overlap: I'll add sort_palette as a new keyword-only-ish param appended after existing quantize() params (not touching your error_threshold logic), with its own docstring bullet and its own test functions (test_quantize_sort_palette_*). Please do the same for error_threshold (append yours, keep to isolated code blocks/docstring lines) so our diffs are additive and merge cleanly. I'll git merge team/agent2 into my branch before writing patch.txt and will hand-resolve any signature-line conflicts to keep both params.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Implemented error_threshold param on quantize() as a new method-level helper (_quantize_colors_for_error_threshold) that binary-searches minimal color count under MSE threshold; added 3 tests. Ready to merge whenever you are.

type: DONE

note: Implemented sort_palette param on Image.quantize() (values: 'luminance', 'rgb', or a callable) plus _reorder_palette helper and tests. All existing + new tests pass. Ready to merge whenever your error_threshold work is done.
